practices.practice: Cloze-in-real-sentence as the atomic unit
practices.what: The core card is a fill-in-the-blank inside a full natural sentence (not an isolated word↔translation card); the learner supplies the one missing word by MC or typing.
practices.why: Tests the word in the exact syntactic/collocational frame it lives in — the central evidence for the 'testing effect in context' over isolated recall.
practices.applicability: This IS our moat: our cloze is built from a sentence of the very book being read (he_niqqud with ____, ru translation revealed on answer). Strictly more contextual than Clozemaster's Tatoeba sentences.

practices.practice: MC↔typed as two explicit difficulty tiers of the SAME item
practices.what: Multiple Choice (pick 1 of 4) and Text-Input are selectable modes for the identical sentence; typing is the harder production tier, MC the easier recognition tier.
practices.why: Recognition is appropriate for fresh items, production for mature ones; offering both lets difficulty scale without new content.
practices.applicability: We auto-escalate by mastery (new/l1/l2→MC, l3/l4/known→typed) rather than letting the user pick — arguably a better default for our level axis.

practices.practice: Automatic MC→written escalation driven by mastery, not user choice
practices.what: Learn shows easy question types (MC, flashcard) early and surfaces harder types (written/typed) more often as you answer correctly; the system, not the user, escalates.
practices.why: Removes the temptation to stay in easy recognition mode; forces production once competency is demonstrated, maximizing retrieval strength.
practices.applicability: Directly mirrored in our l1–l4 mapping. Validates our 'escalation by level' approach as the modern standard.

practices.practice: Adaptive item selection that prioritizes your weak items
practices.what: Learn uses past performance to identify the most challenging items and weights the session toward them, building a personalized study path toward 'mastery'.
practices.why: Spending retrieval effort where memory is weakest is more efficient than uniform coverage.
practices.applicability: We rank active items [l1–l4 by freq] + new, and inject ~2 known for refresh — frequency-weighted, but not yet weighted by per-item error history. A lightweight 'recently-missed first' weight would close the gap.

practices.practice: Time-based spaced intervals with a tunable desired-retention target
practices.what: FSRS schedules each card's next review at the moment recall probability decays to a chosen target (default 90%, range 0.7–0.99); higher retention = shorter intervals and ~2x reviews at 95%, ~4x at 97%.
practices.why: Optimal-timing review (just before forgetting) is the highest-leverage retention mechanic in the entire field; the retention/workload tradeoff is explicit and user-controlled.
practices.applicability: HIGH-value gap. Our SRS is in-session level-floors only (no calendar). A 'due today' queue keyed to last-correct timestamp per word_status would be the single biggest retention upgrade. Explicitly in our v2-backlog ('интервалы по времени').

practices.practice: Cross-session 'due today' queue
practices.what: Reviews persist across days; each day surfaces only the cards whose interval has elapsed, capped to a daily limit.
practices.why: Turns one-off study into a sustained forgetting-curve-fighting habit; the queue is the unit of daily engagement.
practices.applicability: We have no persistent due-queue (sessions are ad-hoc per text). Feasible offline: store nextDue per lemmaKey in word_status, query due items across all read texts. Pairs with cross-text recall (also v2-backlog).

practices.practice: Graded self-report buttons (Again/Hard/Good/Easy)
practices.what: On reveal the learner rates recall difficulty; the rating feeds the scheduler's difficulty/stability estimate, not just binary right/wrong.
practices.why: Captures partial knowledge and confidence that binary scoring misses, improving interval accuracy.
practices.applicability: We score binary (correct/incorrect→±1 level). A 4-button rating is heavier UX and may be overkill for reading-native flow; a lighter 'too easy / got it / hard / no idea' could feed both level movement and future intervals. Optional.

practices.practice: Leech detection and auto-suspend
practices.what: A card lapsed ≥N times (default 8) is tagged a 'leech' and auto-suspended/flagged, on the principle it is too complex or context-poor to keep grinding.
practices.why: Prevents a few unlearnable items from consuming disproportionate session time and demoralizing the learner.
practices.applicability: We have a manual 'ignore' status (transparent in text) but no automatic lapse counting. Adding a lapse counter that suggests 'ignore?' after repeated misses would protect session time. Low effort given word_status exists.

practices.practice: Audio/answer on the BACK of the card only
practices.what: Pronunciation and the full answer live on the reveal side; the prompt side withholds anything that would give away the response.
practices.why: If audio plays the target word during the question, the retrieval is contaminated (recognition, not recall).
practices.applicability: We explicitly play 🔊 only on reveal, never during the question — exactly this invariant. Worth keeping as a hard rule when we add a listening mode.

practices.practice: Mixed exercise types within one review (MC + typing + listening + audio-recognition)
practices.what: A review session interleaves flashcards, MC, typing, and listening drills rather than repeating one format.
practices.why: Format variety prevents pattern-memorization of the card rather than the word, and exercises multiple retrieval routes.
practices.applicability: We interleave MC and typed by level, but not listening/audio-recognition formats yet. Listening cloze is in our v2-backlog; it would add a second retrieval route from the same baked text.

practices.practice: Speed Review under time pressure
practices.what: A timed mode forces fast recognition of already-seen items, sharpening fluency/automaticity rather than first-time learning.
practices.why: Retrieval speed (automaticity) is a distinct skill from accuracy and predicts real reading fluency.
practices.applicability: Plausible later for 'known/l4' refresh (fast recognition of mature words), but lower priority than time-based scheduling. Note: time pressure conflicts somewhat with our consonantal-typed answers.

practices.practice: Tight session sizing with immediate per-item feedback
practices.what: Lessons are short (~12–20 items) with instant correct/incorrect feedback and a clear progress indicator per session.
practices.why: Bounded sessions reduce drop-off; immediate feedback is required for the testing effect to consolidate the correct trace.
practices.applicability: We use N=12 + 'Ещё', k/N progress, and immediate reveal — squarely in the modern band. Good as-is.

practices.practice: Adaptive difficulty model (Birdbrain) predicting per-item correctness
practices.what: A model estimates learner ability and per-exercise difficulty; wrong answers lower ability estimate and raise item difficulty, steering which items appear.
practices.why: Keeps the learner in the desirable-difficulty zone (i+1), neither bored nor overwhelmed.
practices.applicability: We approximate i+1 via frequency ranking and level, not a learned ability/difficulty model. A per-item θ is in our v2-backlog ('сложность по θ'); a simple Elo-style item difficulty would be a lightweight offline step before full FSRS.

practices.practice: Word-bank (tap-to-assemble) as a low-floor production tier between MC and free typing
practices.what: Some production exercises give a scrambled bank of word tiles to tap into order, instead of free keyboard entry.
practices.why: Lowers the motor/orthography barrier of typing (esp. on mobile) while still requiring production-level retrieval.
practices.applicability: HIGH relevance for Hebrew/RTL on mobile: free Hebrew typing is a real barrier (we flag 'on-screen Hebrew keyboard' in v2-backlog). A tap-the-letters/tap-the-word-tiles tier between MC and typed would fit our escalation ladder and dodge the keyboard problem.

practices.practice: Five review activity types over the same lesson vocabulary (flashcards, reverse, cloze, dictation, MC)
practices.what: Words from the lesson are reviewable as flashcards, reverse flashcards, cloze, dictation, and MC, configurable per activity.
practices.why: Same words, multiple retrieval directions (recognition, production, listening-to-spelling) deepen the trace.
practices.applicability: We have cloze + MC + typed from the reading text. Dictation (type what you hear) and reverse (RU→HE production) are natural additions reusing our baked audio + offline resolver.

practices.practice: Status advances only after correct twice in a row (with status-4 fast-track)
practices.what: A LingQ's status auto-bumps when tested correctly twice consecutively in flashcard/cloze/MC; high statuses need only one correct to advance.
practices.why: A double-correct gate filters lucky guesses (esp. in 4-way MC where 25% is chance) before promoting an item.
practices.applicability: We promote +1 on a single correct. Given MC has 25% guess rate, a 'two-correct-in-a-row to leave MC tiers' gate would harden promotions without changing our floor logic. Cheap, high-value robustness fix.

practices.practice: Word status colours that visibly clear from the reading surface as you learn
practices.what: Blue=unknown, yellow=learning, white/cleared=known; the reading page repaints as statuses change, so the 'wall of unknown' visibly recedes.
practices.why: Ties review directly to a visible reading-comprehension payoff — the strongest motivator for reading-native learners.
practices.applicability: We already do this (known=transparent, l1–4=amber, ignore=plain; reveal→setWordStatus→repaint). This is core to our loop and matches the best-in-class reader pattern.

practices.practice: Multi-level term status including an explicit 'ignored' state
practices.what: Terms carry unknown/learning/learned/well-known/ignored; 'ignored' removes proper-noun/irrelevant items from testing.
practices.why: An ignore state keeps names and noise out of the SRS so they don't pollute scoring or distractor pools.
practices.applicability: We have new/l1–4/known/ignore and flag nameSuspect in collectReviewItems. Confirms our level granularity and ignore semantics are standard for reading tools.

practices.practice: Massive-context cloze deletion (MCD): show N surrounding sentences around the blank
practices.what: Test settings can display several sentences of surrounding context around the deleted term, not just the one host sentence.
practices.why: Richer disambiguating context for hard/homograph items; closer to real reading.
practices.applicability: We pick the single occurrence sentence with the most tokens (best single-sentence context). Optionally extending to ±1 sentence from readerRows would help Hebrew homographs and matches our 'morpho-honest' goal — fully offline.

practices.practice: Export reading vocabulary to Anki as cloze cards
practices.what: Terms/sentences export into Anki decks pre-formatted for cloze, handing scheduling to a mature SRS.
practices.why: Lets reading tools defer the hard scheduling problem to Anki/FSRS while owning capture.
practices.applicability: We already ship Anki .apkg export (universal). Until we build native intervals, Anki export is our 'borrowed FSRS' path — worth surfacing from the recall sheet.

practices.practice: Active-mode cloze = guess the original word from context + its translation
practices.what: Active cards show the cloze sentence plus the translation; the learner must produce the original target word.
practices.why: Forces production toward the target language from meaning + context — the hardest, most durable retrieval.
practices.applicability: Our typed tier is exactly this (gloss hint + sentence, produce the Hebrew word, accept consonantal form OR lemma). The form↔lemma acceptance is a Hebrew-specific superset Readlang can't do.

practices.practice: Frictionless auto-capture: every word you look up becomes a review card
practices.what: Each in-reader translation lookup is silently saved to a personal SRS deck, organized by frequency/difficulty.
practices.why: Zero-effort capture maximizes deck coverage; review draws only from words the learner actually met.
practices.applicability: We collect ALL confident lemmas from the text (collectReviewItems) and the session draws from statuses, but capture is per-text, not a persistent personal deck across everything read. Cross-text persistence (v2-backlog) would complete this.

practices.practice: Single-blank cloze with typed answer + audio, real-time pace adaptation
practices.what: Each card is a sentence with one blank; you type the word, hear native audio, and the algorithm adapts pace/selection in real time to your responses.
practices.why: Combines context, production, and listening with continuous difficulty calibration.
practices.applicability: We have typed cloze + audio-on-reveal; pace adaptation is frequency/level-based, not response-time/real-time. Our content advantage: Lingvist's blanks come from curated sentences; ours from the actual text.

practices.practice: Turn any text/photo into a cloze deck (Custom Decks)
practices.what: Users paste/photograph/upload text and the app auto-generates cloze flashcards from it.
practices.why: Personalization to the learner's own material drives relevance and motivation.
practices.applicability: This is our default, not a feature: every session is auto-generated from the book in front of the reader via the offline resolver. We are the 'custom deck from your text' product, with morphology Lingvist lacks.

practices.practice: Vocabulary auto-enrolls into a Review tab after each lesson with a decay metric
practices.what: Newly seen words flow into 'Your vocabulary'; a decay+SRS metric resurfaces them, and AI 'Smart Review' targets exactly what needs revisiting.
practices.why: Closes the learn→review loop automatically and times resurfacing by predicted decay.
practices.applicability: Our loop is read→mark→recall, but resurfacing is in-session/freq-based, not decay-timed. The decay metric is essentially the FSRS gap above; Smart-Review-style targeting = our 'recently-missed weighting' gap.

practices.practice: Varied controlled-practice item types (gap-fill, MC, dictation, sentence reordering)
practices.what: Review mixes gap-fill, MC, dictation, and reorder-the-sentence exercises.
practices.why: Sentence reordering tests word order/syntax, a dimension pure cloze misses.
practices.applicability: We do gap-fill + MC. Sentence-reordering of a baked Hebrew sentence (tap tiles into order) would test RTL syntax and reuse the same tokens — and doubles as the mobile-typing-avoidance tier (see Duolingo word-bank).

practices.practice: Strict 5-minute, no-typing, tap/swipe/match micro-sessions
practices.what: Sessions are hard-capped (~5 min) and built entirely from tapping, matching, and swiping — deliberately no keyboard.
practices.why: Removes typing friction and uses a scarcity timer to drive focus and daily return.
practices.applicability: Partial relevance: the no-typing philosophy supports adding a tap-based tier for Hebrew (keyboard-free), but the 5-min timer/gamified scarcity clashes with our calm reading-native ethos. Borrow the tap-input idea, not the timer.

practices.practice: Word↔image association instead of L1 translation
practices.what: Vocabulary is taught via picture pairing rather than native-language glosses.
practices.why: Avoids L1 mediation, building a more direct concept↔word link.
practices.applicability: Low applicability: our corpus is literary Hebrew (abstract/archaic vocabulary) where images rarely apply, and our value is L1 gloss + morphology provenance. Skip.

practices.practice: Frequency-ordered introduction (most useful words first)
practices.what: New items are introduced in order of statistical real-world frequency/importance (Speakly: top ~4,000; Lingvist: frequency-driven).
practices.why: Maximizes comprehension gain per word learned, prioritizing high-coverage vocabulary.
practices.applicability: We rank both active and new items by corpus frequency (freq desc). Aligned. Our twist: frequency within the actual text being read, so payoff is immediate to that reading.

practices.practice: Listening-first context exercises with real (non-robotic) audio
practices.what: Listening drills paired with sentence drills replay the same useful patterns; audio quality (real clips) is treated as a feature.
practices.why: Listening retrieval is a separate channel; reusing the same sentences across read+heard reinforces faster.
practices.applicability: We have 🔊 (GCP WaveNet→browser) but only on reveal; a true listening-cloze (hear sentence, supply missing word) is v2-backlog. Reuses baked canon audio; high fit for Hebrew where vowel/audio mapping is hard.

practices.practice: Cloze-deletion of grammar points (not just vocab) with hand-written hints
practices.what: Fill-in-the-blank targets a grammar form/construction, with crafted hints, inside example sentences under SRS.
practices.why: Extends the cloze mechanic from lexicon to morphology/syntax — exactly where reading comprehension breaks down.
practices.applicability: Strong fit for our morphology engine: we could cloze a binyan/inflected form and test the form↔lemma transformation (we already accept either). A 'produce the correct inflected form' tier is a uniquely-Hebrew grammar-cloze only our offline morphology enables.

practices.practice: Offline-capable SRS quiz bank
practices.what: The full quiz/SRS bank works offline (e.g. Bunpo's offline JLPT tests).
practices.why: Reliability without connectivity; review anywhere.
practices.applicability: We are offline-first by construction (readerRows + offline resolver + cached audio fallback). Parity with or ahead of these apps.

practices.practice: Per-round configurable new:review ratio and translation visibility
practices.what: Pro users set how many review vs new sentences per round (even 0 new), and whether the translation is visible / shown-after / hidden.
practices.why: Lets the learner tune the recognition-aid level and the learn/review balance to their goal.
practices.applicability: We hide ru until reveal (good default) and fix the known-refresh injection at ~2; neither is user-configurable. A simple 'more new / more review' and 'hint on/off' toggle would add agency cheaply.

practices.practice: Per-item Hard/Easy/0%/100%/Ignore manual overrides + AI 'Explain'
practices.what: On any item the learner can force mastery up/down, mark Hard/Easy, Ignore it, or open an AI explanation of the sentence's grammar.
practices.why: Manual overrides respect learner judgment the algorithm can't see; on-demand explanation resolves the confusion that caused the miss.
practices.applicability: We have 'Подробнее'→openWordCard (root/binyan/POS/gloss/provenance) on reveal = our morphology 'explain', plus manual status via the card. We lack a one-tap Hard/Easy override inside the recall flow and a sentence-level (not word-level) explanation.

practices.practice: Typo tolerance on typed answers (accept near-misses)
practices.what: Typed-answer modes accept minor spelling slips / accent differences rather than failing an essentially-correct response.
practices.why: Punishing trivial typos as 'wrong' corrupts the difficulty signal and frustrates learners.
practices.applicability: We accept consonantal matches (final→medial normalization) of FORM or LEMMA — a Hebrew-appropriate tolerance (ignores niqqud). We do NOT do edit-distance, so a one-letter consonantal slip still fails. A 1-char Levenshtein grace could be added carefully (risk: Hebrew minimal pairs).

practices.practice: First-exposure teach-before-test
practices.what: A brand-new word is shown/taught (with meaning, audio) before it is ever quizzed, so the first encounter isn't a guaranteed failure.
practices.why: Testing an utterly unseen item yields a guess, not retrieval; a brief teach step seeds the trace.
practices.applicability: For 'new' words we go straight to 4-way MC (recognition, gloss hint shown) — softer than free recall but still untaught. Since the word literally appears in the text the user just read, exposure is implicit; an optional 'peek/teach' on new items would make the first test fair.

practices.practice: Gamification scaffolding (streak, XP, daily goal)
practices.what: Streaks, XP, and daily goals create extrinsic return-habit pressure around sessions.
practices.why: Drives daily-active retention; the habit loop is what keeps SRS reviews actually happening.
practices.applicability: In our v2-backlog ('стрик/геймификация'). Lower priority and partly at odds with the calm reading ethos, but a minimal 'words reviewed today / kept-up streak' is cheap and supports the daily due-queue once time-based scheduling exists.

practices.practice: 'I don't know' / Again button distinct from a wrong answer
practices.what: A skip/'don't know' control lets the learner reveal the answer without guessing, scheduling the item as not-yet-known rather than failed.
practices.why: Honest 'no idea' is better signal than a forced random guess and avoids false MC successes.
practices.applicability: Our 4.3b doc specifies MC/typed + check→reveal but does not mention a 'show answer / I don't know' control. Worth confirming in code and adding: on MC it converts a guess into honest no-recall; feeds the same soft-floor demotion.

practices.practice: Morphologically-honest distractor generation
practices.what: MC distractors are chosen to be plausible-but-wrong: same POS, similar length, same root family / homograph readings, drawn from the same lesson's vocabulary — deterministically (seeded).
practices.why: Trivially-wrong distractors let learners eliminate by surface cues without knowing the word; honest distractors force real discrimination.
practices.applicability: Our strongest moat: pickDistractors does sameRoot ≫ samePOS+near-length > samePOS, all from the same text's confident lemmas, seeded for determinism. Generic apps cannot do root-family/homograph distractors without a Hebrew morphology engine — we can. Keep and feature it.

findings.observation: During the QUESTION the always-present 🔊 row-speak button plays the FULL sentence including the target word, speaking the answer aloud.
findings.detail: renderTrainItem() adds a `data-train-rowspeak` button (lines 908) whose handler (line 606) calls speakWord(_trainSession._built.sentence). `sentence` = the full he_niqqud row (set in _trainBuildCloze, line 884), which still contains the blanked word. So tapping 🔊 on the question voices the exact answer — defeating active recall. This directly contradicts the plan's own invariant (BRR_EPIC4_3B line 43: «🔊 на ответе … во время вопроса не играем — выдало бы»). Owner asked for row-audio, but it should play the cloze (before+after) or be answer-on-reveal only, not the whole sentence with the target.

findings.observation: buildCloze blanks only the wordOffset-th occurrence; if the target word appears again in the same sentence the other instance stays visible in before/after, leaking the answer.
findings.detail: buildCloze (lines 1598-1606) concatenates every token except index ti into before/after. A sentence like «אמר ... אמר» blanks one אמר but the other remains in `after`, so the learner just reads/copies the answer. No de-dup or mask of other surface-identical word tokens. Pedagogical correctness bug for recall.

findings.observation: occ.wordOffset is captured from the he-column span index but the cloze is tokenized from he_niqqud — if the two columns tokenize to different word sequences the WRONG word is blanked.
findings.detail: _scanWords reads data-w-offset from spans returned by _studyWordSpans which PREFERS the he column (reader-morph.js lines 1514-1518, 1561). _trainBuildCloze builds the cloze from `data.he_niqqud || data.he` (library-ui.js line 880) and calls buildCloze(tokenize(he_niqqud), wordOffset). he and he_niqqud are separate readerRows fields; any difference in punctuation/maqaf/word count (maqaf U+05BE is a separator per isWordChar lines 41-46) shifts the index, so buildCloze blanks a different word — silently producing a wrong question or leaving the real target visible. There is NO guard that the blanked token's skeleton equals item.surface.

findings.observation: _trainBuildCloze accepts any non-null cloze without verifying the blanked token actually equals the item being trained.
findings.detail: _trainBuildCloze (lines 874-887) picks the occurrence with the most word tokens and takes cz if buildCloze!=null. It never checks stripNiqqud(cz.answer) === item.surface skeleton. So on any offset drift (previous finding) the MC «correct» option (built.cz.answer) and the distractor seed (item) refer to DIFFERENT words. A do-no-harm guard (skip occurrence / try next when skeleton mismatches) is absent.

findings.observation: pickDistractors returns up to n=3 but can return fewer; MC then shows <4 options with no top-up, trivializing or breaking the choice.
findings.detail: pickDistractors (lines 1620-1641) slices uniq to n; on a small/low-confidence text the confident-lemma pool may yield 0-2 distractors. renderTrainItem (library-ui.js line 917-924) builds opts = [answer].concat(distractors) with no minimum, so a 1-2 button MC is possible. Rotation uses opts.length so it won't crash, but a single-option (all-correct) MC is degenerate. No fallback pool (e.g. other-text frequent words) and no guard to fall back to typed mode when distractors<3.

findings.observation: The correct MC option shows the inflected sentence-form (vocalized), while distractors show lemma dictionary forms — both a niqqud-presence and a grammatical-fit tell.
findings.detail: Line 918 sets the answer option `he = built.cz.answer` (the exact vocalized form from he_niqqud); distractors (line 919) use `d.niqqud || d.surface` (lemma form, possibly consonantal if d.niqqud empty). Result: (1) if any distractor lacks niqqud it renders bare consonants while the answer is fully pointed → the pointed one stands out; (2) only the answer is correctly inflected for the blank's slot, so a learner picks by grammatical fit, not lexical recall. R10 moat intent (morpho-honest distractors) is undercut by the form asymmetry.

findings.observation: Items skipped because no usable cloze still count toward s.total, deflating the final «correct / total» score.
findings.detail: renderTrainItem (lines 894-895): on built==null it does s.idx++ and recurses WITHOUT touching s.correct or s.total. s.total = items.length (line 859). Summary shows s.correct + ' / ' + s.total (line 1013), so e.g. 2 unbuildable items make a perfect run read «10 / 12». Skips correlate with the offset/alignment bug, so this masks that failure as user errors. Fix: filter buildable items up front or decrement total on skip.

findings.area: raw status codes shown to user
findings.observation: The reveal level-move line and reveal logic display raw internal status codes (new, l1, l2, l3, l4, known) instead of the localized labels used everywhere else.
findings.detail: checkTrainAnswer builds moved = item.status + ' → ' + next (line 965) and renderTrainReveal prints it verbatim (lines 983, .room-train-moved). item.status/next are raw codes. The study LIST localizes these (STUDY_STATUS_OPTS lines 557-560: new=«новое», l1='1' … known=«знаю», ignore=«игнор»), so the user sees «new → l1» in training but «новое»/«1» in the list — inconsistent and exposes internal vocabulary. No i18n mapping applied to moved.

findings.observation: recollectStudy (list collector) resumes after its await and re-renders the list body even if the user has since switched to train mode, clobbering the training screen.
findings.detail: recollectStudy guards only `if (!_studySheet || _studySheet.hidden) return;` (line 804) — it does NOT check _studyMode before calling renderStudyControls()/renderStudyBody() (lines 807-808). startTraining DOES check `_studyMode !== 'train'` after its await (line 857). So opening the sheet (roomOpenStudyList→recollectStudy in flight) then quickly tapping «🎯 Тренировка» (setStudyMode→startTraining) can let the later-resolving recollectStudy paint the list over the training UI. Add a `_studyMode !== 'list'` bail to recollectStudy after the await.

findings.area: full ru translation shown during MC question
findings.observation: The whole row translation is displayed as a question prompt, which for short sentences hands the learner the meaning of the blanked word on top of the gloss.
findings.detail: renderTrainItem shows gloss (line 911) AND built.ru full sentence translation (line 914, .room-train-ctxq) during the question. Combined with the gloss this is heavy scaffolding for recognition; for a 3-4 word sentence the ru translation effectively names the target. Owner requested context, but consider gating the full ru behind a «показать перевод» reveal for mature (typed) levels, keeping only the gloss for the question.

findings.observation: Correct/incorrect verdict and level move are rendered without any aria-live region, so screen-reader users get no spoken feedback.
findings.detail: renderTrainReveal (lines 972-991) appends .room-train-verdict / .room-train-moved as static nodes; no role=status / aria-live=polite on the body or reveal. The verdict («✓ Верно»/«✗ Неверно») is conveyed by colour + text only. Add an aria-live region for the verdict.

findings.area: a11y — focus not managed across MC and reveal
findings.observation: Switching to train mode and answering an MC do not move focus; after answering the focused option button is disabled, dropping keyboard focus.
findings.detail: renderTrainItem focuses the input only in typed mode (line 933); MC mode leaves focus on the previously-clicked mode-toggle button. onTrainOption disables the clicked option (line 943) so focus lands on a disabled element / body. renderTrainReveal does not focus «Дальше →». Keyboard users must hunt with Tab each step. Only Escape (close) and Enter-in-input (submit) are wired (lines 611-612); arrow-key option selection / Enter-to-advance on reveal are absent.

findings.observation: Deterministic rotation = idx % opts.length makes the correct option land in the same slot for the same item every replay, so «🎯 Ещё» trains position memory.
findings.detail: Line 921 rot = s.idx % opts.length; ordered = opts.slice(rot)+opts.slice(0,rot). Across «Ещё» sessions the item ordering is deterministic (collectReviewItems sorts by freq, line 1590) so item idx and thus answer position repeat. A seed mixing the lemmaKey (not just idx) would keep determinism while varying placement between sessions. Minor.

findings.observation: pickDistractors dedups by lemmaKey, not by displayed surface, so two options can render identical Hebrew text (homographs / form==lemma), one marked correct.
findings.detail: Dedup loop keys on c.lemmaKey (lines 1627-1628). Two distinct lemmaKeys can share a display form (homograph readings keyed separately, or a distractor lemma surface equal to the inflected answer cz.answer). The grid would then show two buttons with the same text; if one equals the answer it is also a valid choice yet scored wrong. Consider de-duping options by displayed (niqqud-stripped) form against the answer text.

findings.observation: new→l1 advances a word but is not counted in the «↑ N уровней» summary because the regex excludes l1.
findings.detail: checkTrainAnswer line 964: levelUps increments only when next matches /^(l[2-4]|known)$/. A correct answer on a `new` word (the most common case) moves new→l1 (nextLevel, reader-morph.js line 1609) but is excluded, so the summary understates progress. Either include l1 or document the intent.

findings.observation: renderTrainItem builds an `ans` object that is never used.
findings.detail: Line 916 `const ans = { lemmaKey, surface, niqqud: built.cz.answer, root, pos };` is never referenced; opts (lines 918-919) are built inline from item/built directly. Dead code — remove.

findings.observation: checkTrainAnswer mutates item.status in place; items and pool share object references with the collected `all` array.
findings.detail: Line 966 item.status = next mutates the object that also lives in s.pool (pool = all, items filtered from all — same references, startTraining line 859). pickDistractors ignores status so no current bug, but it is a latent foot-gun if future distractor logic reads status. Lemmas are unique per session (collectReviewItems dedups by lemmaKey) so no double-counting today.

findings.observation: _normHe strips niqqud + finals but does not handle attached proclitics, so a learner typing the lemma without the sentence's prefix is rejected.
findings.detail: _normHe (line 830) strips niqqud and normalizes final letters, then onTrainSubmit accepts val === _normHe(cz.answer) || val === _normHe(item.surface) (line 954). If the sentence form carries a proclitic (e.g. וְהָאִישׁ → cz.answer skeleton «והאיש») and item.surface is the bare lemma «איש», a learner typing «האיש» (with article, no vav) matches neither → false negative. Geresh/gershayim (U+05F3/4) are not in the niqqud strip range (reader-morph isWordChar lines 46, NIQQUD_RE line 50) so they survive on both sides (consistent), but acronym/abbrev forms could still mismatch. Consider accepting any inflected surface of the lemma from the pool.

findings.area: distractor pool quality vs name suspects
findings.observation: pickDistractors draws from all confident lemmas including nameSuspect words, so a possible proper name can appear as a distractor.
findings.detail: collectReviewItems returns every confident lemma incl. nameSuspect (reader-morph.js line 1592); pickDistractors does not filter nameSuspect (lines 1620-1641). A «возможно имя» token (e.g. יהודית) could surface as a distractor, which is odd as a vocabulary choice and may itself be a mis-resolution. Low impact but inconsistent with the list's hide-names affordance.

findings.observation: When items exist but none produce a cloze, the summary still says «Готово: 0 / N» rather than the «no words» message.
findings.detail: renderTrainSummary shows the trainEmpty message only when s.total===0 (line 1007). If buildTrainSession returned N items but every one was skipped (built null), s.total=N>0 so it shows «Готово: 0 / N» with «🎯 Ещё», implying the user failed all N when in fact nothing was askable. Tie this to the skip-counting bug.

findings.observation: Training UI uses no entrance/pulse animations, and scrollIntoView on reveal is unobtrusive — reduced-motion is effectively respected.
findings.detail: .room-train-* styles (lines 945-985) define no @keyframes/animation/transition; the study card transition is already disabled under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce (line 899). rev.scrollIntoView({block:'nearest'}) (library-ui.js line 991) defaults to instant. No motion-accessibility issue in the training surface.

findings.observation: The blank uses an inline-block with dashed underline and transparent text inside a dir=rtl cloze — renders as a gap on the correct side, but the literal underscores are exposed to screen readers.
findings.detail: .room-train-blank (line 950) sets color:transparent + border-bottom dashed; visually a blank. The cloze container is dir=rtl (library-ui.js line 903) so bidi places before/blank/after correctly for Hebrew. But the span text is literally ' ____ ' (library-ui.js line 905), which a screen reader announces as underscores; an aria-label like «пропуск» would be cleaner. Visual rendering itself is fine.

findings.observation: Rapid double activation is guarded synchronously, so no double scoring or double DB write occurs.
findings.detail: onTrainOption/onTrainSubmit both early-return on _trainSession.answered (lines 936, 948); checkTrainAnswer sets s.answered=true synchronously before any await (lines 959-960). MC buttons are also disabled and the input is disabled after submit. The status write is further guarded by next!==item.status (line 963). Concurrency on answering is sound.

findings.observation: _built is reassigned for every rendered item and only read for the current idx, so no stale-built leak across questions.
findings.detail: renderTrainItem sets s._built before rendering an askable item (line 896); skips (built null) advance idx and recurse without using the old _built; reveal/onTrainCard read s._built for the same idx (lines 975, 994). Summary does not use _built. No mismatch between item and _built was found.

findings.title: Question-side 🔊 reads the WHOLE sentence — including the answer word
findings.rationale: The cloze question renders a row-audio button (library-ui.js:908 `data-train-rowspeak`) whose handler (library-ui.js:606) calls `speakWord(_trainSession._built.sentence)`, and `_built.sentence` is the FULL he_niqqud row WITH the target word in it (set at library-ui.js:884). So a learner can tap 🔊 during the question and hear the missing word pronounced. This directly contradicts the plan's own locked default ('🔊 на ответе … во время вопроса не играем — выдало бы') and the Anki 'audio on the BACK only' invariant the digest flags as a hard rule. Retrieval is contaminated (recognition, not recall) — it breaks the central testing-effect premise of the whole epic.
findings.change: Remove the 🔊 row button from `renderTrainItem` question state, OR make it speak only `built.cz.before + ' … ' + built.cz.after` (skip the blanked token), OR gate it to appear only inside `renderTrainReveal` (where a sentence-🔊 is legitimate). The reveal already has its own answer-🔊 (library-ui.js:980), so question-side audio can simply be dropped.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure UI removal in library-ui.js; no resolver/parity/Anki impact. Deterministic. Add a reader-morph/UI smoke asserting the question DOM contains no element that can synthesize the answer surface.

findings.title: Machine niqqud shown as graded truth with no provenance marker
findings.rationale: The plan's locked R11 default says 'машинный никуд помечать'. In the trainer the vocalized cloze sentence, the MC option vocalizations, and the revealed answer (`built.cz.answer`, library-ui.js:979) all carry niqqud that for baked corpus rows is frequently MACHINE-derived (Nakdan/baked), yet nothing badges it as derived≠asserted. The learner is implicitly taught a vocalization that may be a machine guess, and (for MC) is graded against it visually. Authority-control (R9) and do-no-harm (R11) require the derived vocalization not to be presented as ground truth.
findings.change: Thread the existing provenance signal (the same one the study list/card use, provenanceLabel) into `collectReviewItems`/`_built` and render a small 'никуд машинный' badge on the reveal vocalization (and optionally a subtle marker on the cloze) when the row's niqqud is derived. Typed grading is already consonantal (library-ui.js:954 `_normHe` strips niqqud) so no scoring change is needed — this is a labeling fix only.
findings.invariantRisk: Display-only; consonantal grading already niqqud-agnostic, so oracle-independence and do-no-harm hold. Room-only. No new resolver call — reuse the label already computed in resolveCore.

findings.title: Repeated lemma in the chosen sentence leaves a visible twin of the answer
findings.rationale: `buildCloze` (reader-morph.js:1598) blanks only the single wordOffset-th token. Hebrew literary prose (R7) frequently repeats a root/word in one sentence; if the target word occurs twice in the picked sentence, the un-blanked occurrence sits in `before`/`after` and literally shows the answer. `_trainBuildCloze` even biases toward the LONGEST sentence (library-ui.js:884), which is the most likely to contain a repeat. Contaminated retrieval again.
findings.change: In `buildCloze`, after locating the target token, also blank any other word token whose niqqud-stripped/final-normalized skeleton equals the answer's (mask all surface twins). Alternatively, in `_trainBuildCloze` prefer occurrences whose sentence contains exactly one instance of the surface. Keep it pure/Node-testable and add a smoke case with a repeated word.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure function change with a dedicated unit test; deterministic. No resolver/parity touch.

findings.title: MC distractors length/form-matched to the CITATION form, not to the displayed clitic-inflected answer
findings.rationale: `pickDistractors(item, …)` scores near-length against `item.surface` (the citation/representative form, reader-morph.js:1623), but the option actually displayed as correct is `built.cz.answer` — the real in-sentence token, which can carry proclitics (וכש־, ה־, ל־) and inflection, making it visibly longer/different. Distractors are drawn as bare lemma/first-occurrence forms (`d.niqqud||d.surface`, library-ui.js:919). The net effect: the correct option is often the one with attached clitics / agreement that fits the frame, a syntactic tell that lets a learner pick it without knowing the word. This silently erodes the morpho-honest-distractor moat the epic is built on.
findings.change: Match distractors against the DISPLAYED answer: pass the cloze answer surface (clitic-inflected) length to `pickDistractors`, and where possible surface distractors in a comparable inflected form (same proclitic / same number-gender) using the offline resolver, not bare citation forms. At minimum, strip shared proclitics from the displayed answer so all four options are morphologically parallel.
findings.invariantRisk: Distractor generation stays offline/deterministic from the in-text pool + resolver; no Anki, no parity change. Add a smoke asserting the correct option's clitic profile is not unique among the four.

findings.title: Distractor pool is in-text only; root-family moat degrades silently on short texts
findings.rationale: The pool is `collectReviewItems(mount,…)` — only the confident lemmas of the OPEN text (library-ui.js:856). For a short work the pool may hold a handful of lemmas, so `pickDistractors` falls through sameRoot→samePOS→'any', producing unrelated, easily-eliminated options — and there is no honesty signal that the 'morpho-honest' promise wasn't met. The plan's R3 lens explicitly said 'дистракторы из rootIndex', which is not wired in.
findings.change: When in-text same-root/same-POS candidates are insufficient, augment the pool from the global root index / corpus-vocab (same root family, same POS) so distractors stay morphologically honest regardless of text length. Cap and de-dupe by lemmaKey; keep deterministic ordering.
findings.invariantRisk: Read-only use of existing offline rootIndex/vocab datasets; Room-only; deterministic sort preserved. No resolver-core change.

findings.title: Two-correct-in-a-row gate before leaving the MC (recognition) tiers
findings.rationale: Promotion is +1 on a SINGLE correct (reader-morph.js:1611 `nextLevel`), but new/l1/l2 are 4-way MC with a 25% guess rate — a lucky guess promotes a word toward 'known' and clears it from the reading wall. The digest's LingQ practice ('status advances only after correct twice in a row', flagged 'cheap, high-value robustness fix') exists precisely to filter MC guesses. This guards the do-no-harm integrity of the status overlay that repaints the reader.
findings.change: Track an in-session consecutive-correct counter per lemmaKey (or persist a tiny streak in word_status meta); require two consecutive corrects to advance OUT of an MC level (new/l1/l2). Typed levels (l3+) keep single-correct since guess rate is negligible. Keep the existing soft-floor downgrades unchanged.
findings.invariantRisk: Status still flows only through `setWordStatus`/word_status (no Anki cards); change is in `nextLevel` policy + a counter. Add nextLevel/gate unit tests for all transitions.

findings.title: Proper-name suspects can become cloze targets and distractors
findings.rationale: `collectReviewItems` carries `nameSuspect` (reader-morph.js:1592) and the study LIST offers a 'hide names' filter, but `buildTrainSession` (library-ui.js:832) does NOT exclude name-suspects from training targets, and the distractor pool includes them too. Testing a learner on a character's proper name (R6/R7: names are noise, not vocabulary) wastes session time and pollutes scoring — exactly the rationale behind the Lute/LWT 'ignored' state in the digest.
findings.change: Exclude `nameSuspect` (and explicit `ignore` status) items from training TARGETS in `buildTrainSession`; optionally exclude name-suspects from the distractor pool too, or at least never let a name be the correct option. Surface the same soft 'возможно имя' hint on reveal if one slips through.
findings.invariantRisk: `nameSuspect` stays a soft flag — it must NOT change resolveCore/colour/notes (R11). Filtering happens only in the session builder. Deterministic.

findings.title: 'Longest sentence' occurrence pick is harder, repetition-prone, and stale across sessions
findings.rationale: `_trainBuildCloze` always selects the occurrence with the MOST word tokens (library-ui.js:884). The longest sentence is the most syntactically complex (often a multi-clause run-on where the target's local collocation is buried), the most likely to contain a same-root cue or a repeated twin, and — being deterministic — it is the SAME sentence every session for a given word, inviting sentence-memorization rather than word recall (Memrise/Quizlet warn against format/item memorization). It also conflicts with graded-reading (R8) desirable-difficulty: the richest context is not the clearest cue.
findings.change: Prefer a 'clean' occurrence: enough context (e.g. ≥4–6 words) but penalize sentences containing a same-root word or a surface twin; rotate among eligible occurrences by a seed derived from (lemmaKey, session-counter) so re-runs vary the sentence. Keep it deterministic given the seed.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure selection logic over the existing occ list; deterministic with an explicit seed (no Math.random). No parity/resolver impact.

findings.title: Distractor set is fixed forever — spec promised a per-item seed that was dropped
findings.rationale: The plan's locked default and reader-morph header both say distractors are seeded by item-index, but the shipped `pickDistractors(answer,pool,n)` (reader-morph.js:1620) takes NO seed and is a pure score/freq/lemmaKey sort, so the SAME word yields the SAME three distractors every session. Combined with fixed option rotation (`rot = s.idx % opts.length`), a repeat learner memorizes the option SET rather than the word — the pattern-memorization failure the Memrise/Quizlet practices warn about. It is also a spec-vs-impl drift worth reconciling.
findings.change: Either restore a seed parameter (seed = hash(lemmaKey, sessionCounter)) that deterministically samples among the top morpho-honest candidates (not just the global top-3), or document the intentional removal and rotate the candidate window per session. Keep determinism (seeded, no Math.random).
findings.invariantRisk: Deterministic-with-seed preserves the no-Math.random invariant and reproducible smoke tests; pool stays offline. Update the plan/header to match whichever choice is made.

findings.title: Requeue / weight just-missed items instead of dropping them
findings.rationale: Wrong answers floor the level (reader-morph.js:1610) but the item is not re-queued within the session, and 'Ещё' loads the next freq-ranked portion — so a freshly-missed word may not reappear for a long time. The digest's Quizlet/Duolingo adaptive-selection practice (weight the session toward weak items) and Anki's lapse focus both target this. For a reading-native loop, re-testing the just-missed word a few items later is the highest-leverage cheap retention gain.
findings.change: In the session controller, on an incorrect answer push the item back into the queue a few positions ahead (spaced, capped to avoid loops), and/or weight the next session's ranking by recent-miss. Lightweight, no scheduler needed.
findings.invariantRisk: Session-local array manipulation; deterministic; no word_status schema change beyond the existing level. Room-only.

findings.title: Full sentence translation shown during the typed (production) question
findings.rationale: On the harder typed tiers (l3/l4/known) the question shows both the lemma gloss AND the full row ru translation (library-ui.js:914). For a production task this is the textual analogue of the very 'audio gives it away' concern the digest elevates to an invariant: the learner can back-translate the answer from the full sentence translation, demoting production to recognition. Recognition cue is appropriate for fresh MC items, less so for mature typed items.
findings.change: On typed tiers show only the lemma gloss as the cue and reveal the full sentence translation on the answer side (it is already rendered in `renderTrainReveal`, library-ui.js:984); keep the full translation as a question cue for MC tiers if desired.
findings.invariantRisk: UI-only conditional on `isMcLevel`; deterministic; no parity impact.

findings.title: MC options can mix vocalized and bare-consonantal text → a visual tell
findings.rationale: Option text is `d.niqqud || d.surface` (library-ui.js:919); when a distractor lacks niqqud it renders bare while the answer and other options are vocalized. A learner can pick the 'odd one in/out' by vocalization density rather than meaning, and an unvocalized option also looks 'wrong' regardless of content — undermining distractor honesty.
findings.change: Normalize vocalization across all four options: either show all bare-consonantal (matching the consonantal grading model) or ensure all four are vocalized (fill missing niqqud or strip the answer's). Simplest honest choice: render MC options consonantally so vocalization carries no signal.
findings.invariantRisk: Display normalization only; consonantal display is consistent with the consonantal typed-grading invariant. Deterministic.

findings.title: Same-root word in the surrounding context cues the answer
findings.rationale: Root repetition is a hallmark of Hebrew prose (R7). If `before`/`after` contains another word from the answer's root, the morphological family visibly cues the blank — the learner recalls the root from context, not the target word. This is distinct from the exact-twin case (#3): a different inflection of the same root.
findings.change: In occurrence selection (`_trainBuildCloze`), down-rank sentences whose context tokens resolve to the same root as the target (reuse the offline resolver/root field already on pool items); only fall back to a same-root context if no cleaner occurrence exists.
findings.invariantRisk: Selection-only; uses root already present on items (no extra oracle); deterministic. Room-only.

findings.title: No cross-session due-queue (time-based scheduling) — biggest acknowledged retention lever
findings.rationale: Status moves are in-session level-floors only (reader-morph.js:1609-1614); there is no last-correct timestamp or 'due today' queue, so the loop cannot fight the forgetting curve across days. The digest ranks optimal-timing review (FSRS 'due today') as the single highest-leverage retention mechanic, and the plan's own v2-backlog lists 'интервалы по времени'. Without it the trainer is a per-text drill, not a sustained habit.
findings.change: Add a lightweight offline FSRS-lite: store `lastCorrectAt`/`nextDue`/lapse-count per lemmaKey in word_status (additive migration), and offer a cross-text 'к повторению сегодня' queue. Phase it after the honesty fixes above. (Acknowledged backlog — log as the priority gap, not a regression.)
findings.invariantRisk: Additive word_status fields only — still NOT Anki cards; Room-only; deterministic clock input. Needs db:migrate + backup; gate the new schema with a smoke.

findings.title: Lapse counter → suggest 'ignore' for leeches
findings.rationale: There is no lapse counting; a context-poor or unlearnable word can be missed indefinitely, each miss merely flooring the level. Anki's leech detection (auto-suspend after N lapses) protects session time and morale. The project already has an `ignore` state, so the missing piece is only the counter.
findings.change: Increment a lapse counter on wrong answers in word_status; after N misses (e.g. 6–8) surface a one-tap 'пометить «не учить»?' on the reveal. No auto-suspend without consent (do-no-harm).
findings.invariantRisk: Additive counter on existing word_status; user-consented transition to existing `ignore`; no Anki, Room-only, deterministic.

findings.title: MC can render fewer than four options with no guaranteed-count fallback
findings.rationale: `pickDistractors` returns up to n from the pool with no floor; on a tiny text it can return 0–2, so the MC may present just the answer or a 2-way choice — trivially guessable, breaking the recognition-tier design. There is no fallback to keep four plausible options.
findings.change: Guarantee 4 options: if in-text distractors are short, top up from the global vocab (same POS first) per finding #5; if still short, fall back to the typed tier for that item rather than showing a degenerate MC. Add a smoke asserting MC always renders exactly 4 distinct options or escalates to typed.
findings.invariantRisk: Offline top-up + deterministic; Room-only. No resolver-core change.

findings.title: Empty/degenerate session shows a bare '0 / 0' instead of guidance
findings.rationale: If no items have usable occurrences (`buildTrainSession` returns empty, library-ui.js:846), the session is `total:0` and falls straight to the summary showing '0 / 0' — a dead-end with no explanation, contrary to the R4 'no dead-ends' invariant. A reading-native learner needs to be told to mark some words first.
findings.change: When `items.length === 0`, render an empty-state in the train body ('отметьте слова в списке, чтобы тренироваться' / switch to 📋 Список) instead of the score summary.
findings.invariantRisk: UI-only; Room-only; deterministic; i18n ru/en/he.

findings.title: Typed tiers (l3/l4/known) are untrainable on mobile — no Hebrew input method
findings.rationale: isMcLevel() routes l3/l4/known to a free-text Hebrew <input> (onTrainSubmit, _normHe compares Hebrew). A reading-native user on an Android/iOS phone with only RU+EN keyboards literally cannot type ивритские буквы → the entire production half of the escalation ladder is a dead end exactly for the mature words the loop most wants to harden. This is the single most-cited mobile barrier in the digest (Duolingo word-bank).
findings.change: Add a tap-the-tiles production tier between MC and free typing: shuffle the answer's letters (or a small word-bank of the answer + distractor surfaces) as tap-into-order tiles, reusing built.cz.answer and pickDistractors. Make free-typing an opt-in for users who have a Hebrew keyboard; default mobile to word-bank. Keep _normHe for validation.
findings.invariantRisk: Room-only; pure-core already exposes buildCloze/pickDistractors — add a deterministic letter-shuffle helper (seed=item index, no Math.random) to reader-morph.js, Node-tested. No resolver/Anki/parity impact.

findings.title: nameSuspect words enter the training session with wrong glosses
findings.rationale: _scanWords sets nameSuspect via NAME_HINT and the 4.3a list shows a «возможно имя» badge + hide filter, but buildTrainSession() filters only by occ + status — it never excludes nameSuspect. So homograph-names (יְהוּדִית→«еврейский», צְבִי→«газель», עַלִּיזָה→«весёлый», the exact 3 surfaced in the 4.3a measure) become cloze items whose prompt gloss is wrong → the learner actively memorizes a falsehood and gets level-penalized for it. R11 do-no-harm violation through the back door.
findings.change: In buildTrainSession exclude (or hard-deprioritize) items where x.nameSuspect, mirroring the list's hide-names default; optionally surface a «не слово для изучения» skip on reveal that sets ignore.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure UI filter over the already-collected flag; resolver/coloring untouched (NAME_HINT stays SOFT). Deterministic.

findings.title: No «Не знаю» / skip control — MC forces a blind guess with 25% lucky promotion
findings.rationale: Once an MC item renders, the only paths are pick-an-option (which auto-commits +1/−1) — there is no honest «I don't know» that reveals without a lucky 25% promotion, and no skip for an item the learner can't engage. The brief explicitly lists I-don't-know/skip as a missing control; without it nextLevel promotes new→l1 on a coin-flip guess, polluting the status axis the reader repaint depends on.
findings.change: Add a «Не знаю» button on the question (both MC and typed) → routes to checkTrainAnswer(false) but with a neutral floor (no demotion, no promotion) so honesty isn't punished; add a «Пропустить» that advances without scoring. Reveal the answer either way.
findings.invariantRisk: Reuses checkTrainAnswer/nextLevel; add a neutral branch to nextLevel (pure, Node-tested). Room-only.

findings.title: Require two-correct-in-a-row to leave MC tiers (anti-lucky-guess gate)
findings.rationale: nextLevel promotes on a single correct, but MC has a 25% guess floor (often higher when distractors are sparse) — so a word can climb new→l1→l2 on luck and then be force-escalated to typed before it's actually known. LingQ's double-correct gate (digest: «cheap, high-value robustness fix») filters this without changing the floor logic.
findings.change: Track a per-item consecutive-correct counter in _trainSession.items (or a streak field in word_status) and only apply the +1 in MC tiers after two consecutive corrects; keep single-correct for typed tiers.
findings.invariantRisk: Counter lives in session/word_status, not Anki; nextLevel stays pure with an extra arg, Node-tested. Deterministic.

findings.title: Missed items are not re-queued within the session
findings.rationale: On a wrong answer the item is demoted and disappears until a future session; the testing-effect payoff of immediately re-attempting a just-missed item (Duolingo «strengthen», Anki re-show) is lost. With N=12 and many fresh items, a learner can finish a session having seen every miss exactly once.
findings.change: In checkTrainAnswer, on incorrect push a shallow copy of the item to the end of s.items (cap re-queues to 1 per item) and bump s.total; show it again later in the same session before summary.
findings.invariantRisk: Session-local array mutation only; no DB churn for the re-attempt (commit status on final outcome). Deterministic ordering preserved.

findings.title: Level-up counter under-reports progress (new→l1 not counted)
findings.rationale: checkTrainAnswer increments s.levelUps only when next matches /^(l[2-4]|known)$/, so a new→l1 promotion — the most common and most motivating event for a frontier-heavy session — is never celebrated in the «↑ N уровней» summary. The most successful outcomes feel invisible.
findings.change: Count any upward move: increment levelUps whenever correct && next !== item.status && _LV_UP[item.status] === next (i.e., promotion regardless of band).
findings.invariantRisk: One-line counting fix in library-ui; no engine/DB change.

findings.title: MC distractor morphology mismatches the cloze frame — a grammatical tell, not a meaning test
findings.rationale: The correct option is built.cz.answer (the inflected form in THIS sentence), while distractors render d.niqqud||d.surface (each distractor's own first-occurrence form). In a vocalized RTL sentence with gender/number agreement visible, a reading-savvy learner picks the option that grammatically fits the frame rather than the one that means the right thing — undermining the R10 morpho-honest-distractor moat that is the whole point.
findings.change: Either (a) present all four options in a comparable surface form (e.g., all base/lemma niqqud, blank shown lemma-neutral) or (b) prefer distractors whose pos+inflection features match the target; at minimum equalize the visible morphological 'shape' so selection is meaning-driven.
findings.invariantRisk: Lives in renderTrainItem option assembly + pickDistractors scoring; deterministic, resolver-read-only.

findings.title: MC degenerates to <4 options on sparse texts
findings.rationale: pickDistractors returns up to n from the same-text confident pool with no padding; a short poem or a screen with few confident lemmas yields 1–2 distractors → a 2- or even 1-option MC that is trivially guessable (or a single 'choice'), silently inflating correct-rate and triggering false promotions.
findings.change: Guard in renderTrainItem: if distractors.length < 3, pad from inflected forms of the same root/POS, then from the global frequency list, else fall back to the typed/word-bank tier for that item; never render an MC with <4 options.
findings.invariantRisk: Padding pool stays deterministic (sorted, seeded); fallback reuses existing typed path. Room-only.

findings.title: No cross-session time-based «due today» queue (highest-leverage retention lever)
findings.rationale: Scoring is in-session level-floors only; nothing schedules a word for review tomorrow/next week. The digest and our own v2-backlog flag FSRS-style interval review as the single biggest retention upgrade. Without it, a word studied today is only re-seen if the learner happens to reopen the same text — the forgetting curve is never fought across days.
findings.change: Add lastCorrectAt + nextDue to word_status (migration), set on checkTrainAnswer via a simple level→interval table (l1=1d…known=long), and add a «На сегодня» session source that queries due lemmaKeys across read texts. Ship interval-lite before full FSRS.
findings.invariantRisk: New word_status columns, not Anki cards; deterministic interval table (no scheduler RNG). Keep it Room-local; cross-text needs bodies (stage with current-text first).

findings.title: Leech detection → suggest «игнор» after repeated misses
findings.rationale: A few unlearnable/context-poor items (often mis-resolved names or rare hapax) can be missed every session, consuming session slots and demoralizing — Anki auto-suspends leeches. We already have an ignore status and the do-no-harm floors, but no lapse counting, so a leech grinds forever.
findings.change: Add a lapse counter to word_status; after N (e.g., 6) cumulative misses, surface a one-tap «Слишком трудно? → 🚫 игнор» on the reveal; never auto-suspend silently.
findings.invariantRisk: Counter in word_status; suggestion is user-confirmed (no silent state change). Deterministic.

findings.title: Weight the session toward recently-missed items, not pure frequency
findings.rationale: buildTrainSession ranks active items by freq-desc only; Quizlet/Duolingo weight sessions toward your weakest items. A word you missed last session should resurface before a never-missed high-freq one — retrieval effort is best spent where memory is weakest.
findings.change: Incorporate a lightweight recency-of-miss boost (from the lapse/lastResult field) into buildTrainSession ordering ahead of raw freq; keep freq as tie-break.
findings.invariantRisk: Ordering only; deterministic given stored fields. Room-only.

findings.title: «Ещё» replays the same items in the same MC positions → pattern memorization
findings.rationale: buildTrainSession is freq-desc deterministic and MC placement is rot=s.idx%opts.length, so re-running (data-train-again) tends to re-show overlapping items with the answer in the same slot — Memrise's explicit warning: the learner memorizes the card layout, not the word. Determinism (R11) here works against varied practice (R4).
findings.change: Make placement vary deterministically per-attempt (seed = item.lemmaKey + sessionNonce derived from a persisted attempt count, still no Math.random) and have «Ещё» advance to the next chunk of the frontier rather than rebuilding the same head.
findings.invariantRisk: Deterministic seed (documented), Node-testable; no RNG. Room-only.

findings.title: No macro-progress against the 973-word frontier → endless, no closure
findings.rationale: The 4.3a measure found 973 new words on one text; training serves 12 at a time with a summary of «X/12» and «Ещё», but no «изучено сегодня / осталось M» or daily goal. With no sense of dent or completion, a motivated reader can't tell if they're 1% or 50% through, which kills the sustained-habit value the loop is for.
findings.change: Show macro-progress in the summary (e.g., «сегодня: 24 слова · в этом тексте осталось 949») and an optional daily goal; derive remaining from collectReviewItems counts by status.
findings.invariantRisk: Read-only aggregation over existing collect results; per-day count persisted locally. Deterministic.

findings.title: No gamification (streak / daily goal / completion celebration)
findings.rationale: Market-parity expectation (Duolingo streak, Memrise points): the summary is a bare «X/N» with no streak, no daily-goal ring, no celebratory beat. For a self-directed offline reader the streak+goal is the cheapest retention/engagement multiplier and is already in our v2-backlog.
findings.change: Add a local daily-streak + daily-goal counter (words reviewed today) with a light completion state in renderTrainSummary; keep it offline-local and non-dark-pattern (no loss-aversion nags).
findings.invariantRisk: Local-only counters, no server; deterministic display. Room-only.

findings.title: No session-length control (fixed N=12)
findings.rationale: TRAIN_N is hard-coded 12; the brief lists session-length among missing controls. Different users / time budgets want 5 (quick) or 20 (deep). A fixed length is fine as a default but offering 5/10/20 reduces drop-off and respects context (commute vs. desk).
findings.change: Add a small length segmented control (5/10/20) in the train header feeding TRAIN_N into buildTrainSession; persist the choice.
findings.invariantRisk: Trivial param; deterministic. Room-only.

findings.title: Full RU sentence translation is shown in the QUESTION for typed/production tiers — over-scaffolds
findings.rationale: renderTrainItem always shows built.ru (room-train-ctxq) on the question side, in addition to the lemma gloss. For production tiers (typed/word-bank) the full sentence translation hands the learner the missing word's contextual meaning, contaminating recall toward recognition; Anki's discipline is answer-side context only.
findings.change: For typed/word-bank tiers, show only the lemma gloss on the question and move built.ru to the reveal (room-train-ctx, already present); keep full translation on the question for easy MC tiers if desired.
findings.invariantRisk: Display conditional on isMcLevel; no scoring/engine change. Deterministic.

findings.title: applyDecorations() repaints the whole text on every single answer
findings.rationale: checkTrainAnswer nulls readerWordStates and calls applyDecorations() (full repaint of the painted reader, up to ~1769 rows) after EVERY answer, even though the sheet covers the reader. On large texts this is per-item jank for an invisible repaint and wasted battery.
findings.change: Defer the repaint: invalidate state per-answer but debounce applyDecorations to session end / sheet close (or rAF-coalesce), since the reader behind the sheet isn't visible mid-session.
findings.invariantRisk: State invalidation still immediate so the eventual repaint is correct (cross-surface key parity preserved); just throttle the paint. Room-only.

findings.title: No recourse when the item is a resolver mis-gloss — status auto-commits before reveal
findings.rationale: checkTrainAnswer commits setWordStatus before renderTrainReveal, so if the prompt gloss is a mis-resolution (name as content, homograph), the learner is penalized and the wrong status persists with no «это не то слово / ошибка распознавания» escape. Pairs with the nameSuspect leak above.
findings.change: On reveal add «не изучать это слово» → revert the just-applied level move and set ignore; show the «Подробнее» (openWordCard) so the user can see the real analysis before deciding.
findings.invariantRisk: Revert uses item._from already stored; sets ignore via setWordStatus. No resolver change; oracle-independent.

findings.title: Add reverse (RU→HE) and listening/dictation activities over the same items
findings.rationale: LingQ offers five retrieval directions over one lesson's words; we have HE-blank cloze + MC + typed only. Reverse (gloss→produce HE) and dictation (type/word-bank what you hear, reusing speakWord on the baked audio) add distinct retrieval routes (production, listening-to-spelling) from data we already have, deepening the trace without new content. Audio-only cloze is already in our v2-backlog.
findings.change: Add activity variants reusing collectReviewItems + speakWord: listening-cloze (play built.sentence audio, blank shown) and reverse-production (RU gloss prompt → word-bank). Interleave per Memrise mixed-format practice.
findings.invariantRisk: Audio on the QUESTION must NOT speak the target word in isolation (keep the answer-only 🔊 invariant); play the full sentence only. Deterministic, Room-only, no resolver change.

findings.title: Mid-session dismissal silently destroys the session
findings.rationale: closeStudySheet sets _trainSession=null and Escape also closes; an accidental tap/Escape mid-session loses all in-flight progress (already-committed statuses persist, but the session position/queue is gone) with no confirm or resume. Friction for a deliberate practice flow.
findings.change: Persist a lightweight resume cursor (idx + item keys) so re-opening «Тренировка» offers «Продолжить»; or confirm before discarding a session with answered>0.
findings.invariantRisk: Resume cursor is local/ephemeral; committed statuses are already the source of truth. Deterministic.

findings.title: Polish: number-key MC shortcuts, visual progress bar, RTL blank rendering
findings.rationale: Desktop MC has no 1–4 key shortcuts (Enter is wired only for typed); progress is text-only «k / N» (no bar); and the ` ____ ` blank is LTR underscores spliced into an RTL vocalized sentence (room-train-blank) which can sit visually mis-positioned at 380px. Small premium-feel gaps vs. Quizlet/Duolingo.
findings.change: Add 1–4 keydown handlers for MC options, a thin progress bar fed by idx/total, and verify/正 the blank renders correctly inside the RTL flow at 380px (screenshot light+dark); confirm room-train-opt buttons aren't caught by the global button{width:100%} mobile rule.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure UI/CSS in the Room sheet; screenshot-verify at 380px per project workflow. No engine change.

findings.title: MC distractors are lemma citation-forms, the answer is the inflected sentence token — guessable by form-fit
findings.rationale: In renderTrainItem the correct option is built.cz.answer (the exact vocalized inflected token from the book sentence), while pickDistractors options use d.niqqud||d.surface = the lemma/citation form returned by _scanWords (card.word/card.niqqud). The blank sits in a concrete syntactic slot, so the only option whose form agrees with that slot (gender/number/tense/clitic) is the answer; a learner picks by grammatical fit, not meaning. This silently destroys the testing-effect the moat is built on and inflates the correct-rate, which then drives bogus level-ups.
findings.change: Make distractor display match the answer's morpho-shape: when an MC distractor shares POS, surface-realize it in the same inflection slot as the answer (reuse the resolver/inflection-dict already shipped for the family chips) before showing d; at minimum, when no agreeing form exists, prefer same-length distractors and never show the citation form alongside an inflected answer. Add a smoke assertion that no MC item mixes one inflected option with otherwise-citation options for verbs.
findings.invariantRisk: Stay Room-only and resolver-untouched: realize forms only from the already-shipped offline inflection dataset (no Dicta call), deterministic (pure function of item+pool, no Math.random); if no offline form is available, fall back to current behavior rather than guessing.

findings.title: Missed items are never re-tested in the same session — only the answer is shown, never re-produced
findings.rationale: checkTrainAnswer demotes the word, shows renderTrainReveal, and onTrainNext just does idx++. A failed item is seen (recognition of the revealed answer) but the learner never successfully retrieves it. The testing effect consolidates the SUCCESSFUL retrieval, not the act of reading the answer; Anki's Again and Duolingo's re-queue exist precisely for this. Right now a session can end with 0 successful retrievals of the words the learner is weakest on.
findings.change: On an incorrect answer, push the item back into _trainSession.items a few slots later (e.g. +3, or to the end) instead of dropping it; cap re-queues per item (e.g. 2) so the session still terminates. Count session 'mastered' only on a correct retrieval, and reflect re-queued items in the k/N denominator or a separate 'осталось' counter.
findings.invariantRisk: Deterministic insert position (fixed offset, not random); do-no-harm — re-queue does not re-apply nextLevel demotion twice for the same miss (only the first attempt moves word_status). No Anki cards, no DB writes beyond the existing setWordStatus.

findings.title: Typed tier (l3/l4/known) is unusable without a Hebrew keyboard → systematic wrong demotions of mature words
findings.rationale: isMcLevel routes l3/l4/known to free-text input (data-train-input, dir=rtl). A remote learner on a Russian/English phone has no Hebrew IME, so they cannot type the answer at all and will be marked wrong → nextLevel demotes known→l4, l4→l3, etc. This is a do-no-harm violation: the escalation tier itself manufactures failures unrelated to memory, nuking correct grounded statuses. The v2-backlog already flags 'экранная ивритская раскладка' but the typed tier shipped without it.
findings.change: Before free typing is viable, gate the typed tier behind an input method that does not need an OS Hebrew keyboard — ship the word-bank tap-tiles tier (next finding) as the default production mode, and only offer free-typing when a Hebrew input is detectable or opted-in. Until then, do not demote on an empty/never-submitted typed answer (treat 'no attempt' as skip, not wrong).
findings.invariantRisk: Room-only UI; do-no-harm floor — distinguish 'wrong attempt' from 'could not attempt' so word_status is never lowered by a keyboard gap. Deterministic.

findings.title: Word-bank / tap-the-letters production tier between MC and free-typing (Duolingo)
findings.rationale: Free Hebrew typing is the real motor/orthography barrier on RTL mobile. A tap-to-assemble tier (scrambled letter tiles, or 4-6 word tiles to order) keeps production-level retrieval — the learner must produce the form, not just recognize it — while dodging the keyboard problem entirely. It is the natural middle rung of our new/MC → typed escalation ladder and directly fixes the unusable typed tier.
findings.change: Add a third mode in renderTrainItem for l3/l4 (keep free-typing for 'known' or as opt-in): present the answer's letters (with finals) shuffled deterministically as tap tiles; assembled string checked with the existing _normHe consonantal compare (accept form or lemma). Reuse buildCloze for the sentence; reuse pickDistractors only for MC. Add a new isTileLevel helper alongside isMcLevel.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure deterministic shuffle (seed = item index, like the MC rotation, no Math.random); Room-only; resolver/notes-autogen untouched; consonantal compare keeps R1 niqqud-honesty (niqqud shown only on reveal). Add a Node smoke for the tile-assembly checker.

findings.title: No time-based spacing or cross-session 'due today' queue — SRS is in-session level-floors only
findings.rationale: nextLevel moves a level but nothing records WHEN a word was last retrieved, so there is no optimal-timing review (the single highest-leverage retention mechanic in the field per the FSRS evidence). Sessions are ad-hoc per open text; a word learned today and a word learned a month ago are treated identically, and nothing surfaces words from other already-read texts when they are about to be forgotten.
findings.change: Extend word_status (new migration, additive) with lastSeenAt, nextDue, reps, lapses per lemmaKey; on each retrieval set nextDue via a simple expanding interval keyed to the level (l1≈1d, l2≈3d, l3≈7d, l4≈21d, known≈60d). Add a 'due' ordering/quota to buildTrainSession (due items first) and, as a follow-up, a cross-text due queue keyed by lemmaKey. Start with intervals-by-level (deterministic), defer full FSRS.
findings.invariantRisk: Additive DB column only (db:migrate + db:backup); offline-first (no server, computed from timestamps); do-no-harm — overdue never auto-demotes, it only re-surfaces. Deterministic interval table, no Math.random.

findings.title: Two-correct-in-a-row gate before leaving MC tiers (LingQ) to defend against 25% MC guessing
findings.rationale: nextLevel promotes +1 on a SINGLE correct answer, but MC has a 25% chance floor (worse when the pool is small and <4 options render). A lucky guess on a new word promotes it toward 'known', polluting both the reading-surface paint and any future due-scheduling. LingQ requires two consecutive correct (with a fast-track at high status) precisely to filter guesses.
findings.change: Track a per-item correct-streak in the session/word_status; in the MC tiers (new/l1/l2) require two consecutive correct to advance a level (first correct holds the level, second promotes). Keep single-correct promotion for the production tiers where guessing is not a factor.
findings.invariantRisk: Reuse the additive word_status fields (reps/streak); do-no-harm floors unchanged; deterministic. No Anki cards.

findings.title: No teach-before-test step for brand-new words — 'new' words go straight to recall
findings.rationale: buildTrainSession feeds status==='new' words straight into MC. For a word the learner has never engaged, gloss→pick-the-Hebrew with morpho-honest distractors is a cold guess, not retrieval; Quizlet/Memrise show a word once (recognition/flashcard) before testing it. Cold-testing new items produces noisy first-attempt data and a frustrating floor of guessing.
findings.change: For a word's first-ever appearance in training, insert a no-fail 'see it' step (vocalized word + gloss + 🔊 + the cloze sentence with the word IN place, highlighted) before its first scored MC; mark the word as introduced (reps>0) so subsequent sessions go straight to testing. Alternatively route pure-new through the study-list flashcard and only admit them to recall once status>=l1.
findings.invariantRisk: Room-only; the intro step writes no status (or only an 'introduced' flag), so it cannot demote; deterministic; reuses openWordCard/speakWord. No new resolver work.

findings.title: Session composition has no new-vs-review quota — learning items can crowd out all new words (or vice versa)
findings.rationale: buildTrainSession does active = learning.concat(fresh).slice(0, N-kRefresh). With ≥10 l1–l4 items, ZERO new words ever enter a session, so the frontier is never tested and acquisition stalls; on a fresh text with no learning items it is all-new and the learner is overwhelmed. Modern SLA tools (Duolingo/Anki) explicitly cap new-per-session and guarantee a review/new balance to keep the learner in the i+1 desirable-difficulty band.
