findings.change: Replace the flat concat-slice with explicit quotas, e.g. reserve up to ceil(N*0.4) for new and fill the rest with due/learning (freq-desc), then top up from whichever pool has surplus; keep the known-refresh interleave. Surface the mix so the session is reproducibly balanced.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure function over the collected list, deterministic; Room-only; no schema change required for v1 (pure ordering). Add a Node smoke asserting the quota split on a synthetic item set.

findings.title: Cloze leaks the answer when the target lemma occurs twice in the chosen sentence
findings.rationale: buildCloze blanks only the wordOffset-th word token. If the same surface/lemma appears more than once in the selected sentence (common with high-freq function-ish content words), the OTHER occurrence stays visible and hands the learner the answer verbatim — the retrieval is contaminated to pure copying.
findings.change: In _trainBuildCloze, when scoring occurrences, deprioritize (or post-process) sentences where the answer surface appears more than once; or blank ALL occurrences of the same normalized surface in buildCloze and accept any single fill. Prefer a sentence without a duplicate when one exists (tie-break already exists on context richness).
findings.invariantRisk: Pure deterministic selection over item.occ; Room-only; resolver untouched. Extend the existing buildCloze Node smoke with a duplicate-word case.

findings.title: MC degrades to <4 options (or a trivial single-option test) on small texts
findings.rationale: pickDistractors(item, s.pool, 3) draws from the same text's confident lemmas; a short text or a rare POS yields fewer than 3 distractors, so the MC renders 2–3 buttons (50%+ guess) or, in the limit, just the correct answer — no test at all. On a short Ben-Yehuda poem this is the common case, and it silently promotes words.
findings.change: Guarantee a minimum distractor count by widening the pool when the in-text pool is short — fall back to a frequency-matched lemma set from the shipped corpus-vocab / inflection dataset (POS-and-length matched) so MC always has 4 options; if even that fails, force the production (tile/typed) tier instead of a degenerate MC.
findings.invariantRisk: Offline fallback only (shipped datasets, no network); deterministic ordering; resolver-core untouched. Room-only.

findings.title: Homograph same-root distractor can duplicate the answer's displayed surface (two identical buttons)
findings.rationale: pickDistractors dedupes by lemmaKey, but it scores sameRoot ≫ everything; a homograph reading of the same root can have a niqqud/surface string equal to the answer's vocalized form. Two visually identical option buttons make the item unanswerable/confusing and break the morpho-honest-distractor promise.
findings.change: After selection, dedupe options by their DISPLAYED string (stripNiqqud or full vocalized text), not just lemmaKey, and skip any distractor whose display equals the answer's; backfill from the next-best scored candidate.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure deterministic post-filter inside pickDistractors / the MC builder; resolver untouched. Extend pickDistractors smoke with a homograph-collision case.

findings.title: Full RU sentence translation is shown DURING the MC question, contradicting the locked 'reveal-only' default and over-cueing
findings.rationale: The locked default (plan §Залоченные дефолты) says 'перевод предложения (ru) — на ответе (не выдавать)', but renderTrainItem appends the whole row translation as room-train-ctxq before answering (line ~914, 'owner request'). For MC, gloss prompt + full sentence translation is close to a giveaway and weakens the retrieval demand; it also makes the correct-rate (which drives level moves) too easy.
findings.change: Keep the lemma gloss as the prompt; show the full sentence translation blurred / behind a 'показать перевод' tap during the question (small recall-cost), and unblurred on reveal. This preserves the owner's context affordance without handing over the answer. Document the divergence from the locked default either way.
findings.invariantRisk: UI-only, Room-only, deterministic; no resolver/parity impact. i18n the toggle label (ru/en/he).

findings.title: Machine/baked niqqud on the answer is presented as gold on reveal, unmarked
findings.rationale: renderTrainReveal shows built.cz.answer vocalized from he_niqqud, which on baked corpus rows can be machine niqqud. The plan's own R11 invariant says 'машинный никуд помечать'. Presenting possibly-wrong vocalization as the authoritative answer during the highest-attention moment (reveal) risks teaching an incorrect pointing.
findings.change: Carry a niqqud-source/confidence flag through collectReviewItems→reveal; when the answer's vocalization is machine-derived, mark it (subtle badge/asterisk + tooltip) the same way the study card marks machine niqqud, and prefer the resolver's vocalization over the baked sentence niqqud for the displayed answer when it is higher-confidence.
findings.invariantRisk: Do-no-harm/oracle-independence — do not overwrite a grounded niqqud with a lower-confidence one; mark, don't silently 'correct'. Room-only, deterministic.

findings.title: Leech detection — lapse counter that suggests 'ignore' after repeated misses
findings.rationale: A few unlearnable/context-poor items (often mis-resolved homograph names that slipped past nameSuspect) will keep failing and consume disproportionate session time, demoralizing the learner. Anki auto-suspends leeches; we already have an 'ignore' status and the word_status store, so this is low effort.
findings.change: Increment a lapses counter (the same additive field as the FSRS gap) on each miss; after N (e.g. 4) consecutive/total lapses, surface a one-tap 'отметить «игнор»?' suggestion on reveal. Do not auto-ignore — suggest.
findings.invariantRisk: Additive word_status field; suggestion only (owner/learner decides), no auto status change → do-no-harm. Room-only, deterministic.

findings.title: Weakness-weighted item selection (Quizlet) once lapse history exists
findings.rationale: buildTrainSession ranks purely by frequency (b.freq - a.freq) plus level membership; it does not weight toward items the learner actually keeps missing. Spending retrieval effort where memory is weakest is more efficient than uniform/frequency coverage.
findings.change: Once lapses/lastResult are stored (FSRS gap), add a 'recently-missed first' weight to the active ordering (e.g. sort by lapses desc, then due, then freq). Lightweight, no model needed.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure deterministic ordering over stored counters; Room-only; no resolver impact. Smoke the ordering.

findings.title: Audio-only / dictation recall as a second retrieval route (Memrise/LingQ), reusing baked audio
findings.rationale: All current items are visual (read sentence → pick/type). A listening-cloze (play the sentence audio with the target word muted/blanked, learner types/taps it) exercises a distinct retrieval route (sound→form) that predicts real reading+listening fluency, and reuses our already-shipped speakWord/baked audio. It is in the v2-backlog; flagging as the highest-value next mode.
findings.change: Add a listening variant of the cloze for mature levels: play built sentence audio (existing 🔊 path), hide the blank's text, learner produces via tap-tiles/typing. Reuse _trainBuildCloze + the tile checker.
findings.invariantRisk: Reuse existing GCP→browser TTS fallback (offline-degradable); Room-only; deterministic item selection; no new resolver work. Guard against the known GCP/browser end-detect issue noted in 4.3a v2-backlog.

findings.title: Manually-tracked unconfident words (track-any-word) are excluded from recall
findings.rationale: _scanWords (hence collectReviewItems) admits only exact|likely lemmas. The shipped track-any-word feature lets a learner set l1–known on UNCONFIDENT/surface-keyed words; those statuses paint in the reader but can NEVER enter a training session, so a word the learner explicitly chose to study is silently untrainable. The training set diverges from the tracked set.
findings.change: Include surface-keyed manually-tracked words in collectReviewItems (status != new/undefined) even when resolution is unconfident, using the surface form for the cloze answer and gloss=user-meaning where present; route them to the tile/typed production tier (skip morpho-honest MC distractors since no reliable family) rather than dropping them.
findings.invariantRisk: Key parity — use the SAME statusKeyForCard / surface-key logic as paint+save so collect==paint==save still holds (the cross-surface consistency invariant); honest UX — no fabricated root/family for unconfident items; Room-only, deterministic.

findings.title: Session summary under-counts level-ups (excludes new→l1) and MC answer position is mildly predictable
findings.rationale: Two small fidelity issues that affect the feedback signal: (1) checkTrainAnswer counts levelUps only when next matches /^(l[2-4]|known)$/, so a new→l1 promotion (a real, motivating first acquisition) is never tallied in the 'X уровней' summary; (2) MC placement rot = s.idx % opts.length puts the answer in the first slot on every idx where idx%len==0 (0,4,8…), a learnable position pattern.
findings.change: (1) Count any upward level change (including new→l1) in s.levelUps. (2) Derive the rotation from a stable per-item hash (e.g. lemmaKey) instead of the sequential idx so position is deterministic but not index-correlated.
findings.invariantRisk: Both pure/deterministic (no Math.random); Room-only; trivial smoke updates.

findings.title: Massive-context (±N sentences) toggle for thin or very short cloze sentences (Lute/LWT MCD)
findings.rationale: The cloze is a single sentence; Ben-Yehuda poetry/aphorisms often give a 3–4 word line, which is too little syntactic/collocational frame for the in-context testing effect that is our whole premium claim. Lute/LWT optionally show several surrounding sentences around the blank.
findings.change: When the chosen occurrence's sentence has fewer than a threshold of word tokens, extend the displayed context to the adjacent readerRows row(s) around the blank (the blank still in the target sentence). Make it a quiet default for short sentences rather than a user setting in v1.
findings.invariantRisk: Read-only over readerRows (self-contained, with the existing DOM fallback); deterministic neighbor selection; Room-only; resolver untouched.

findings.title: wordOffset is a HE-column index but the cloze is blanked in the niqqud tokenization → wrong word can be deleted
findings.rationale: In _scanWords the occurrence offset comes from data-w-offset of the HE (consonantal) column span (wrapCellHtml counts wIdx over tokenize(row.he)). But _trainBuildCloze blanks the wordOffset-th WORD token of tokenize(he_niqqud). alignSurfaceNiqqud exists precisely because he and he_niqqud DRIFT (look-ahead recovery up to 3), and corpus niqqud coverage is bimodal (~56.6%). When the two columns tokenize to different word counts/order, the blanked niqqud token is NOT the target lemma: the MC 'correct' option (built.cz.answer) and the reveal answer become a DIFFERENT word than the prompted gloss/item — a confidently-presented wrong answer. This is the worst class of error for a moat that sells morpho-honesty.
findings.change: In _trainBuildCloze, after R.buildCloze(...), assert stripNiqqud(cz.answer) === _normHe(item.surface); if it mismatches, re-locate the target by scanning niqqud word tokens for the one whose skeleton equals item.surface near wordOffset (same look-ahead idea as alignSurfaceNiqqud) and rebuild; if none found, discard that occurrence and try the next. Alternatively carry a niqqud-column wordOffset in occ. Add a Node smoke fixture where he and he_niqqud differ in word count.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure/Room-only: change lives in library-ui _trainBuildCloze + reader-morph buildCloze (Node-testable), resolver core untouched, no Anki, fully deterministic (skeleton match, no RNG).

findings.title: Question-time 🔊 plays the FULL vocalized sentence including the target → recall is contaminated
findings.rationale: renderTrainItem renders a data-train-rowspeak 🔊 button DURING the question, and its handler (library-ui line 606) speaks _trainSession._built.sentence — the complete he_niqqud sentence, i.e. it pronounces the very word the learner must recall. The plan explicitly claims '🔊 на ответе … во время вопроса не играем — выдало бы' and the competitor digest names this exact invariant ('audio on the BACK only; if audio plays the target during the question the retrieval is recognition, not recall'). The implementation violates its own stated invariant: any learner can tap 🔊 to hear the answer.
findings.change: Either remove the row-audio button on the question side (keep 🔊 only in renderTrainReveal, which already exists at line 980), or speak a target-muted variant (blank the answer token in the spoken string). Gate it: only enable question-side audio once s.answered is true.
findings.invariantRisk: Room-only UI change; reuses existing speakWord; deterministic; no resolver/parity/Anki impact.

findings.title: Repeated target surface in the chosen sentence leaks the answer (only one occurrence is blanked)
findings.rationale: buildCloze blanks exactly the wordOffset-th word. If the same lemma surface occurs more than once in the selected sentence (very common with high-frequency content words, which _trainBuildCloze actively prefers because it picks the LONGEST sentence), the other occurrence(s) stay visible in cz.before/cz.after — the answer is printed in plain sight next to the blank. This silently destroys the testing effect for exactly the frequent words the session prioritizes.
findings.change: In _trainBuildCloze, detect other word tokens in the sentence whose skeleton equals the target skeleton; either blank ALL of them (multi-blank cloze, answer still single) or down-rank/skip such occurrences in the richest-context selection. Add a smoke case with a repeated word.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure logic in buildCloze/_trainBuildCloze; deterministic; Room-only; resolver untouched.

findings.title: MC distractor starvation: short texts yield <3 (or zero) distractors → trivial/auto-correct items inflate promotions
findings.rationale: pickDistractors(item, s.pool, 3) is best-effort and can return fewer than 3 when the text has few confident lemmas (and pool excludes by lemmaKey). With 1–2 options guess rate is 50–100%; with 0 distractors the only button shown is the correct answer — an automatic win that still calls nextLevel(correct) and promotes the word. The 4-option assumption in renderTrainItem is never enforced, so on small texts the recall loop hands out free level-ups, corrupting word_status quality.
findings.change: Require a minimum of 3 distractors for MC; if pickDistractors returns fewer, fall back to typed mode for that item (isMcLevel override) rather than showing a degenerate MC. Track and assert min-distractor in smoke.
findings.invariantRisk: Room-only; deterministic fallback (no RNG); does not touch resolver or create Anki cards.

findings.title: Distractors deduped only by lemmaKey, not by displayed surface/skeleton → two visually-identical options, one arbitrarily 'correct'
findings.rationale: pickDistractors skips by c.lemmaKey only. Hebrew homographs are distinct lemmas with the same consonantal skeleton (e.g. סֵפֶר/סַפָּר/סָפַר). A distractor whose vocalization is missing (falls back to d.surface = bare skeleton) or coincides with the answer's vocalization will render an option string-equal to built.cz.answer. The learner then sees two identical buttons; correctness is decided by the data-correct attribute, so picking the visually-correct one can be scored WRONG. This is both unfair and morpho-dishonest.
findings.change: In pickDistractors also reject any candidate whose DISPLAYED string (d.niqqud||d.surface) equals the answer's displayed string, and whose stripNiqqud skeleton equals the answer skeleton when neither carries distinguishing niqqud. In renderTrainItem, dedupe the final option strings before shuffle.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure pickDistractors + Room-only render guard; deterministic; resolver untouched.

findings.title: Distractors are lemma-citation forms, not slot-matched inflections → the only grammatically-fitting option is the answer
findings.rationale: MC options use d.niqqud = card.niqqud, i.e. the lemma's representative/citation form (often infinitive/singular/masc), while the correct option is built.cz.answer, the INFLECTED form taken from the sentence. A morphologically literate learner sees that only one option agrees with the sentence's gender/number/tense slot and picks it without knowing the lexeme — the distractor pool defeats its own morpho-honesty. Vocalization is also inconsistent (inflected-vocalized answer vs citation-vocalized or bare distractor), an additional visual tell.
findings.change: Where possible draw the distractor's surface from one of ITS occurrences in the same text (reuse occ to grab an inflected form), or at least normalize presentation so all four options share the same niqqud-presence. Document that this MC tier tests lemma recognition, and lean on the typed tier for true form production. Add a same-POS/same-slot smoke assertion.
findings.invariantRisk: Uses already-collected occ data; Room-only; deterministic; no resolver change.

findings.title: Require two-correct-in-a-row before promoting out of recognition (MC) tiers (LingQ pattern)
findings.rationale: nextLevel promotes +1 on a SINGLE correct, but MC is 4-way (25% chance, higher under distractor starvation, finding 4). The digest flags LingQ's double-correct gate as the cheap, high-value robustness fix that filters lucky guesses before advancing a word. Without it, word_status — the spine of the whole retention loop and the in-text colour wall — is polluted by guesses, undermining the do-no-harm SRS-lite premise.
findings.change: Track a per-lemma in-session correct streak (transient, in _trainSession); only call nextLevel-up out of new/l1/l2 after two consecutive correct in MC. Single correct still allowed to advance the higher (typed) tiers, matching LingQ's status-4 fast-track. Keep floors on miss unchanged.
findings.invariantRisk: Session-local state only; persistent word_status still uses setWordStatus; deterministic; Room-only; no Anki.

findings.title: Proper-noun (nameSuspect) lemmas are not excluded from the distractor pool or the session
findings.rationale: collectReviewItems returns all confident lemmas with nameSuspect flagged, but buildTrainSession filters only by status and pickDistractors ignores nameSuspect. A name (e.g. from NAME_HINT) can appear as a vocabulary distractor or be trained as if it were a learnable content word — morpho-dishonest, since names aren't part of the productive lexicon and pollute both scoring and the option set.
findings.change: Exclude nameSuspect items from the distractor pool in pickDistractors (or pre-filter s.pool), and optionally from session items unless the user opts in — mirroring the study list's 'hide names' affordance. Add a smoke case with a flagged name in the pool.
findings.invariantRisk: Reuses existing soft nameSuspect flag (never touches resolveCore/NAME_PROPER); Room-only; deterministic.

findings.title: Typed answer-checking rejects valid ktiv male/chaser spelling variants
findings.rationale: onTrainSubmit accepts only _normHe(answer-form) or _normHe(item.surface) — exact consonantal skeletons. Unvocalized Hebrew legitimately varies in plene/defective spelling (extra ו/י), and the learner has no niqqud to disambiguate while typing. A correct word typed with the alternate (but standard) spelling is marked WRONG, demoting the level — a do-no-harm violation that punishes correct knowledge.
findings.change: Add a tolerant comparison for mater-lectionis variation (normalize optional internal ו/י, or accept if the niqqud-stripped forms differ only by added/removed matres). Keep it conservative to avoid over-accepting; cover with smoke (e.g. accept both spellings of a plene word). Show the canonical vocalized form on reveal regardless.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure _normHe/checking change; deterministic; Room-only; resolver untouched.

findings.title: Same-root distractor can be grammatically valid in the blank → genuine multiple-acceptable-answer cloze marked wrong
findings.rationale: pickDistractors scores sameRoot highest (+100). A same-root word in a different binyan/derivation can be syntactically and semantically acceptable in the deleted slot; since correctness is judged by identity, a learner choosing a contextually valid alternative is scored wrong and demoted. This is the classic cloze ambiguity problem, sharpened by deliberately picking the closest morphological neighbour.
findings.change: When a same-root distractor is also a plausible slot filler, prefer ones that clearly clash on number/gender/person (so they are unambiguously wrong in context), or cap same-root distractors to at most one per item. Document the trade-off; this is acceptable as 'desirable difficulty' but should be bounded.
findings.invariantRisk: Pure pickDistractors heuristic; deterministic; Room-only.

findings.title: Correct MC option position is a predictable function of item index (learnable pattern)
findings.rationale: Placement uses rot = s.idx % opts.length with the answer always at index 0 pre-rotation, so the correct position cycles predictably across items. Deterministic is required (no Math.random — good), but a *predictable* correct position is a test-validity leak: a learner can infer the answer slot without reading. Determinism and unpredictability are both achievable.
findings.change: Seed the rotation/shuffle by a stable hash of item.lemmaKey (still deterministic, reproducible in smoke) rather than the sequential idx, so position is decorrelated from item order.
findings.invariantRisk: Deterministic seeded shuffle (no RNG); Room-only; smoke remains reproducible.

findings.title: levelUps summary undercounts: new→l1 promotions are not counted
findings.rationale: checkTrainAnswer counts a level-up only when /^(l[2-4]|known)$/.test(next), so the most common beginner progression new→l1 is never reflected in the end-of-session '↑ N уровней'. Minor but it understates exactly the frontier progress that motivates reading-native learners (the cleared-wall payoff the digest calls the strongest motivator).
findings.change: Count any correct answer where next !== prior status as a level-up (include l1), or report 'words advanced' rather than a filtered subset.
findings.invariantRisk: Cosmetic counter; Room-only; no data/parity impact.

findings.title: Smoke coverage misses every correctness/honesty edge case introduced by 4.3b
findings.rationale: reader-morph-smoke tests buildCloze only on a niqqud-less sentence (he==he_niqqud), and pickDistractors only on a clean pool. None of the failure modes above are gated: he/he_niqqud offset drift (finding 1), distractor starvation (4), identical-option dedup (5), name exclusion (8), repeated-word leak (3), question-audio contamination (2). Per R10 measure-before-code, these must be locked or they will silently regress.
findings.change: Add Node fixtures: (a) row where he has more words than he_niqqud, asserting the blanked token's skeleton == target; (b) pool of size <3; (c) homograph/identical-display distractor; (d) name in pool; (e) sentence with a repeated target. Assert pickDistractors never returns the answer's displayed string and never a nameSuspect.
findings.invariantRisk: Test-only; pure Node helpers already exported; no runtime/parity change.

findings.title: Items with no gloss AND no ru context present an uncued blank ('вспомни слово')
findings.rationale: The recall cue is item.gloss (falling back to a generic 'вспомни слово') plus the row ru translation. If both gloss is empty and the row has no ru, the learner faces a blank with no semantic cue — an unanswerable item that will be missed and demote the word, harming status quality and morale.
findings.change: In buildTrainSession/renderTrainItem, require at least one cue (gloss OR ru); skip or de-prioritize cue-less items, or surface the in-context Hebrew collocation as the cue. Reuse existing fields only.
findings.invariantRisk: Room-only selection logic; deterministic; no resolver/Anki impact.

result.title: Question-time 🔊 plays the full vocalized sentence including the cloze target → audio leaks the answer (recall→recognition)
result.evidence: library-ui.js:908 renderTrainItem renders a `data-train-rowspeak` 🔊 button DURING the question (comment l.900-901: "plays the whole sentence — owner request"). Handler l.606 speaks `_trainSession._built.sentence`; _trainBuildCloze l.884 sets `sentence: sent` where `sent = he_niqqud||he` — the FULL vocalized sentence, including the target. Visual cloze blanks the target (l.904-906 before+____+after) but the spoken string does not → audio reveals the word to be recalled. Plan BRR_EPIC4_3B line 43 states the exact invariant: "🔊 на ответе … во время вопроса не играем — выдало бы". Legit answer-side 🔊 already exists in renderTrainReveal l.980. CAVEAT: question-side audio AND question-side ru translation (l.914, also "Owner request") were both deliberately added by the owner, relaxing the pure-recall invariant on purpose.
result.recommendation: nice-to-have / flag-to-owner: real leak, but the owner explicitly requested question-side audio+translation (relaxing recall-purity). Prefer the candidate's target-muted spoken variant (speak before+after, skip cz.answer) over removing the owner-requested button; confirm with owner before changing.

result.title: Same-root distractor can be a valid cloze filler → genuine answer marked wrong
result.evidence: reader-morph.js:1630-1634 score() gives sameRoot +100 (dominant), sorted top-n at 1636-1641; smoke reader-morph-smoke.js:695 asserts same-root ranked first. Grading is identity-only: library-ui.js:918 marks correct only for answer lemmaKey; onTrainOption (935-945) scores off data-correct flag; onTrainSubmit:954 accepts only cz.answer/item.surface — no alternative-filler acceptance. No cap on number of same-root distractors. Mitigation note: distractors shown in own form (line 919 d.niqqud||d.surface), not re-inflected to slot, so agreement usually already clashes → mostly clearly wrong; truly-ambiguous case (two compatible same-root lemmas confident in one short text) is uncommon.
result.recommendation: nice-to-have — cap same-root distractors to ≤1 per item; low priority since slot-agreement clash already makes most same-root distractors unambiguously wrong.

result.title: Repeated target surface in the chosen cloze sentence leaks the answer (only one occurrence blanked)
result.evidence: buildCloze (public/js/reader-morph.js:1598-1606) blanks ONLY the wordOffset-th word token; lines 1603-1604 concat all other tokens verbatim into before/after, so any duplicate of the answer surface stays visible. _trainBuildCloze (public/js/library-ui.js:874-887) picks the occurrence with the most word tokens (longest sentence, line 884), raising repeat probability; buildTrainSession (library-ui.js:832-847) is freq-desc so high-freq content words (most repeatable) are prioritized. MC option text == built.cz.answer (library-ui.js:918), so a visible duplicate is trivially matched; typed mode can be copied. No dedup/multi-blank in code; plan (BRR_EPIC4_3B...:39,49) only says 'blank n-th word'; smoke (scripts/premium/reader-morph-smoke.js:649-690) tests only a no-repeat sentence. Caveat: function words (the truly repetitive tokens) are excluded from the confident-content pool, so studied-lemma in-sentence repeats are real but less frequent than the rationale states.
result.recommendation: nice-to-have: blank ALL tokens whose normalized skeleton (_normHe) equals the target in buildCloze (single answer, multi-blank) and add a repeated-word smoke case; cheap, pure, Room-only.

result.title: Two-correct-in-a-row gate before promoting out of MC (recognition) tiers
result.evidence: Concern is real: reader-morph.js:1609-1614 nextLevel promotes +1 on a SINGLE correct (_LV_UP), no streak. MC is 4-way (library-ui.js:917-919: answer + pickDistractors(...,3) = 25% guess floor, worse under distractor starvation). checkTrainAnswer (library-ui.js:962-963) persists the +1 via localDb.setWordStatus into word_status (the in-text colour-wall spine) — a lucky guess does pollute persistent status. Not implemented: _trainSession (library-ui.js:859) has no per-lemma streak. BUT the proposed session-local fix is ineffective/harmful: collectReviewItems keys one item per lemmaKey (reader-morph.js:1520-1592) and buildTrainSession (library-ui.js:832-846) slices unique items with NO re-queue of misses (renderTrainItem just s.idx++), so each lemma appears at most once per session. A transient in-session two-in-a-row gate can never trigger a second time → it would block ALL new/l1/l2 promotions, breaking the loop instead of filtering guesses. A genuine LingQ double-correct needs a PERSISTED cross-session streak (extra word_status metadata/migration) — still Room-only/deterministic/no-Anki, but M effort, not the S the candidate implies.
result.recommendation: nice-to-have but REDESIGN: the session-local streak as specified is a no-op (one occurrence per lemma per session, no re-queue) and would block all MC promotions; an effective version needs a persisted cross-session streak counter — defer unless guess-pollution is observed in practice.

result.title: Proper-noun (nameSuspect) lemmas not excluded from cloze distractor pool or training session
result.evidence: reader-morph.js:1592 collectReviewItems passes nameSuspect through but returns all confident lemmas regardless. library-ui.js:832-847 buildTrainSession filters only by status+occ, never nameSuspect. reader-morph.js:1620-1641 pickDistractors scores only on root/POS/length, ignores nameSuspect. The hideNames affordance is study-LIST-only (library-ui.js:637 studyFiltered) and defaults false (line 564/815); startTraining (848-861) re-collects fresh and never reads _studyView.hideNames, so training/distractors ignore names entirely.
result.recommendation: nice-to-have — honor the EXISTING _studyView.hideNames toggle in startTraining for list/train consistency; do NOT exclude names by default (contradicts owner default-show + soft-heuristic false-positive risk); distractor exclusion is optional cosmetic.

result.title: MC distractor starvation: short texts yield <3 distractors → degenerate/auto-correct MC inflates word_status promotions
result.evidence: CONFIRMED in code. reader-morph.js:1620-1642 `pickDistractors` is best-effort: it builds `uniq` (pool minus answer's lemmaKey) and returns `uniq.slice(0, n)` — no minimum guard, so it returns <3 (or 0) when the text has few confident lemmas. library-ui.js:917-925 `renderTrainItem` MC branch: `const distractors = pickDistractors(item, s.pool, 3)` then `opts = [{correct}].concat(distractors...)`; the 4-option assumption is never enforced — with 0 distractors `opts.length===1` and a single (correct) button is rendered. library-ui.js:935-945 `onTrainOption` reads that button → correct=true → checkTrainAnswer(true) → line 962-963 `nextLevel(item.status, true)` → `setWordStatus` PROMOTES. R11 floors exist only on the down path (_LV_DOWN, reader-morph.js:1610), so an undeserved up-level is not floored — a free promotion. Note the pool is the WHOLE text's confident lemmas (library-ui.js:856,859 `pool: all` from collectReviewItems), which for a real reading-room work is large (live-verify showed 973 and 36 items); so true starvation only hits very short / low-niqqud texts with ≤3 confident lemmas — a genuine but uncommon edge. No min-distractor assertion exists in the 4.3b smoke per the plan's gate list (BRR_EPIC4_3B doc: pickDistractors gate checks morpho-priority + determinism + 'no answer', not a floor count).
result.recommendation: nice-to-have: in renderTrainItem require distractors.length>=3 else override to typed mode for that item, and add a min-distractor assertion to smoke; cheap (S), R11-aligned, but real-world frequency is low since pool=whole-text confident lemmas.

result.title: Cloze MC options deduped only by lemmaKey, not by displayed surface/skeleton → two identical buttons, one scored correct
result.evidence: reader-morph.js:1627-1628 pickDistractors skips only by c.lemmaKey (== answer.lemmaKey, and seen[lemmaKey]); never compares displayed string (niqqud||surface). Pool items (collectReviewItems, reader-morph.js:1592) carry first-occurrence surface/niqqud; in unvocalized Ben-Yehuda text niqqud falls back to bare consonantal skeleton (memory: niqqud coverage ~56% bimodal). library-ui.js:918-924 renderTrainItem builds opts = [{he:built.cz.answer,correct:true}, ...distractors.map(d=>({he:d.niqqud||d.surface,correct:false}))], rotates, NO display-string dedup; correctness read from data-correct in onTrainOption (library-ui.js:937). A homograph distractor (distinct lemmaKey, same skeleton, missing/identical niqqud) renders string-equal to built.cz.answer → two identical buttons, one data-correct=1; tapping the look-alike scores WRONG and demotes via checkTrainAnswer→nextLevel(...,false) (library-ui.js:958-963). Caveat: pickDistractors receives `item` (representative form), not built.cz.answer, so the answer-equality check must live in renderTrainItem where built.cz.answer is known; also library-ui.js:916 `ans` is dead (built, never passed).
result.recommendation: do-now (small): in renderTrainItem dedupe final option display strings and drop any distractor equal to built.cz.answer before rotate; add a skeleton-collision guard in pickDistractors; cover with the existing pickDistractors smoke. Real fairness/honesty defect (R11/R10), occasional frequency.

result.title: levelUps summary undercounts: new→l1 promotions are not counted
result.evidence: library-ui.js:964 `if (correct) { s.correct++; if (next !== item.status && /^(l[2-4]|known)$/.test(next)) s.levelUps++; }` — regex matches l2/l3/l4/known but excludes l1. reader-morph.js:1609-1613 nextLevel: _LV_UP={"new":"l1",...} so a correct answer on a `new` word yields next="l1", which is persisted (library-ui.js:963 setWordStatus) but never increments s.levelUps. Summary rendered at library-ui.js:1014 ('↑ '+s.levelUps+' уровней'). The most common beginner progression (new→l1) is thus omitted from the count.
result.recommendation: nice-to-have — one-line fix: count any correct answer where next !== prior status (include l1); cosmetic counter, Room-only, parity/resolver/Anki untouched.
